codepipeline.customactiontype.CfnAbout: The `AWS::CodePipeline::CustomActionType` resource creates a custom action for activities that aren't included in the CodePipeline default actions, such as running an internally developed build process or a test suite. You can use these custom actions in the stage of a pipeline. For more information, see [Create and Add a Custom Action in AWS CodePipeline](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/codepipeline/latest/userguide/how-to-create-custom-action.html) in the *AWS CodePipeline User Guide*.

codepipeline.customactiontype.Category.Description: The category of the custom action, such as a build action or a test action.
codepipeline.customactiontype.Category.Allowed values: Approval | Build | Deploy | Invoke | Source | Test

codepipeline.customactiontype.Configurat.Description: The configuration properties for the custom action.  You can refer to a name in the configuration properties of the custom action within the URL templates by following the format of {Config:name}, as long as the configuration property is both required and not secret. For more information, see [Create a Custom Action for a Pipeline](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/codepipeline/latest/userguide/how-to-create-custom-action.html).

codepipeline.customactiontype.InputArtif.Description: The details of the input artifact for the action, such as its commit ID.

codepipeline.customactiontype.OutputArti.Description: The details of the output artifact of the action, such as its commit ID.

codepipeline.customactiontype.Provider.Description: The provider of the service used in the custom action, such as CodeDeploy.

codepipeline.customactiontype.Settings.Description: URLs that provide users information about this custom action.

codepipeline.customactiontype.Tags.Description: The tags for the custom action.

codepipeline.customactiontype.Version.Description: The version identifier of the custom action.
